Borago (Borago officinalis) is an annual herb belonging to the Boraginaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including the reduction of inflammation and improvement of cardiovascular health. The bioactive constituents of Borago include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Borago include teas, infusions, and oil extracts, which are used to treat various health conditions.
